Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.

<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Execute the planned sequence
actions = ['RIGHT']*3 + ['SPACE'] + ['RIGHT'] + ['DOWN']*2 + ['SPACE'] + ['RIGHT']*3 + ['SPACE']
print(f"Executing {len(actions)} actions: {actions}")
result = action(actions)
print(f"Result: {last_action_result}")

# Check remaining blue objects

blue_objs = [n for n in seg if n['color'] == 'b']
print(f"Blue objects remaining: {len(blue_objs)}")
for n in blue_objs:
    print(f"  boundary={n['boundary']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, children={n['children']}")

# Check player position
for n in seg:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        print(f"Player: boundary={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
